message:
fix(subprocess): kill_on_drop on every timeout-wrapped spawn; drain pipes concurrently

Every place RCH wraps a spawned subprocess in `tokio::time::timeout(...,
cmd.output())` was a latent process+fd leak. When the timeout fires, the
future is dropped — but without `kill_on_drop`, the underlying child
process keeps running detached. For SSH/SCP/rch-wkr invocations that means
the local process holds the network socket open until SSH's own keepalive
eventually gives up (minutes to hours), while tmux/ps show a growing forest
of orphaned children. On a busy hook this compounds into a slow accumulation
that manifests as "daemon restart fixes the issue" weeks later.

Adds `cmd.kill_on_drop(true)` before every `spawn()` / `output()` that sits
underneath a `tokio::time::timeout`:

  rch/src/commands/hook.rs
    hook_test: rch in hook mode — the 30s timeout would otherwise leave
    rch-in-hook-mode processes running detached, possibly holding their
    own daemon slot reservations.

  rch/src/fleet/ssh.rs
    build_ssh_args / build_scp_args: every fleet-wide ssh/scp invocation
    runs under a timeout. Adds kill_on_drop on the shared arg builder so
    every downstream command inherits the guarantee.

  rch/src/hook.rs
    run_worker_ssh_command: replaces `cmd.output()` with an explicit
    spawn + concurrent stdout/stderr drain + child.wait(), all under the
    outer `tokio::time::timeout`. Two independent improvements in one
    rewrite:
      - kill_on_drop ensures cancellation kills the local ssh process
      - concurrent stdout+stderr `read_to_end` via `tokio::try_join!`
        prevents a deadlock when remote output exceeds the OS pipe
        buffer (~64KB). The previous `cmd.output()` drained both pipes
        after `wait()`, which deadlocked verbose remote commands.

  rchd/src/benchmark_scheduler.rs
    execute_benchmark_on_worker: defense-in-depth. The explicit
    `child.kill().await` calls cover timeout + IO-error paths; this
    covers the panic-unwind / task-cancellation paths where the
    explicit cleanup never runs.

  rchd/src/cancellation.rs
    remote kill-script ssh under `remote_kill_timeout`. This is the
    single most embarrassing place to leak an ssh process — we're
    literally in the middle of trying to clean up a stuck build and
    failing to clean up our own cleanup.

No API or behavior change on the happy path. The only observable
difference is that timed-out subprocesses now die promptly instead of
limping along.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -426,11 +426,19 @@ pub async fn hook_test(ctx: &OutputContext) -> Result<()> {
     // Find the rch binary
     let rch_path = std::env::current_exe()?;
 
-    // Spawn rch in hook mode (no arguments = hook mode)
+    // Spawn rch in hook mode (no arguments = hook mode).
+    //
+    // `kill_on_drop` is essential here because the call below wraps
+    // `child.wait_with_output()` in `tokio::time::timeout(30s, ...)`. On
+    // timeout, the future is dropped — without this flag the child rch
+    // process keeps running detached, which for a hook test means leaking
+    // a process that may also have taken its own slot reservation on the
+    // daemon. Force a SIGKILL when the future drops.
     let mut child = Command::new(&rch_path)
         .stdin(Stdio::piped())
         .stdout(Stdio::piped())
         .stderr(Stdio::piped())
+        .kill_on_drop(true)
         .spawn()
         .context("Failed to spawn rch in hook mode")?;
